summary.rationale: The profile set now uses a product-first selected register, keeps brand, dense operations, and music surfaces as explicit alternatives, and removes active rubric dependence on the discarded warm restrained profile.

findings.message: Runtime tone variables and design-tokens.template.json still reflect the prior token baseline, so future rendered UI work needs a token-alignment pass before claiming visual implementation of the new profile.
findings.recommendation: Handle runtime token alignment as a separate browser-verified visual task.
evidence: build.json records that src/app/globals.css was intentionally not changed.
aesthetic-profile.json records runtime token alignment as an open question.

notes: Schema-valid run and JSON assets preserve required design-rule-engine structure.
The new profile set gives clearer routing by surface type.
External skill references are framed as references only, reducing workflow ambiguity.
The selected Product Craft Workbench profile is more suitable for current app/workflow surfaces than the discarded profile.
Candidate profiles are differentiated by use case and register rather than a single mood family.
Runtime token alignment remains an intentional follow-up.

The profile model reflects OpenAI frontend-skill restraint, Anthropic frontend-design subject grounding, Impeccable register separation, and UI UX Pro Max coverage without treating those skills as installed.

next_steps: Run a separate browser-verified visual task if runtime tokens or rendered UI should be changed to express the new selected profile.
